name: Changeover Matrix Optimizer
description: Industrial-grade tool for analyzing and optimizing changeover times between product families using Lean/SMED principles and Six Sigma methodologies.

inputs.label: Changeover Time Matrix (minutes)
inputs.businessContext: Average time to switch from one product to another within the same production line. Based on SMED (Single-Minute Exchange of Die) standards.

businessContext_i18n.en: Average time to switch from one product to another within the same production line. Based on SMED (Single-Minute Exchange of Die) standards.

inputs.label: Number of Changeovers per Month
inputs.businessContext: Total changeovers performed monthly across all shifts. Used to calculate total changeover downtime.

businessContext_i18n.en: Total changeovers performed monthly across all shifts. Used to calculate total changeover downtime.

inputs.label: External Setup Time per Changeover (minutes)
inputs.businessContext: Setup activities that can be performed while the machine is running (e.g., tool preparation). Target for SMED optimization.

businessContext_i18n.en: Setup activities that can be performed while the machine is running (e.g., tool preparation). Target for SMED optimization.

inputs.label: Internal Setup Time per Changeover (minutes)
inputs.businessContext: Setup activities that require the machine to be stopped. Primary target for reduction via SMED.

businessContext_i18n.en: Setup activities that require the machine to be stopped. Primary target for reduction via SMED.

inputs.label: Number of Product Families
inputs.businessContext: Distinct product groups processed on the line. Higher counts increase changeover complexity.

businessContext_i18n.en: Distinct product groups processed on the line. Higher counts increase changeover complexity.

inputs.label: Standard Deviation of Changeover Time (minutes)
inputs.businessContext: Measure of variability in changeover times. Used for Six Sigma process capability analysis.

businessContext_i18n.en: Measure of variability in changeover times. Used for Six Sigma process capability analysis.

inputs.label: Shift Pattern
inputs.businessContext: Operating schedule affecting available production time and changeover frequency.

businessContext_i18n.en: Operating schedule affecting available production time and changeover frequency.

inputs.label: SMED Implementation Status
inputs.businessContext: Indicates whether Single-Minute Exchange of Die principles have been formally applied.

businessContext_i18n.en: Indicates whether Single-Minute Exchange of Die principles have been formally applied.

inputs.label: Target Changeover Time (minutes)
inputs.businessContext: SMED target for changeover time reduction. Typically <10 minutes for world-class performance.

businessContext_i18n.en: SMED target for changeover time reduction. Typically <10 minutes for world-class performance.

rules.message: Sum of internal and external setup times cannot exceed total changeover time.
Target changeover time may be unrealistic without SMED implementation.
At least 2 product families required for matrix optimization.

thresholds.message: Changeover time exceeds 60 minutes. Immediate SMED analysis recommended.
Coefficient of variation >30%. Process is unstable. Apply Six Sigma DMAIC.
External setup ratio below 20%. Opportunity to convert internal to external tasks.

formulas.name: Total Changeover Downtime per Month
formulas.description: Total production time lost due to changeovers.

formulas.name: Internal Setup Ratio
formulas.description: Proportion of changeover time that is internal (machine stopped).

formulas.name: SMED Potential Savings
formulas.description: Maximum time reduction achievable by converting internal to external tasks.

formulas.name: Process Capability Index (Cpk) for Changeover Time
formulas.description: Six Sigma capability measure for changeover time consistency.

formulas.name: Changeover Frequency Impact on OEE
formulas.description: Impact of changeover downtime on Overall Equipment Effectiveness (OEE) availability.

formulas.name: Optimized Changeover Time (with SMED)
formulas.description: Projected changeover time after applying SMED improvements.

formulas.name: Annual Cost Savings from Changeover Reduction
formulas.description: Estimated annual cost savings based on reduced downtime and labor.

primary.label: Changeover Optimization Score
primary.description: Composite score (0-100) indicating overall changeover efficiency based on SMED maturity, variability, and target achievement.

hiddenLossDrivers.description: High internal setup time indicates lack of SMED standardization.
hiddenLossDrivers.impact: Reduces machine availability by up to 40%.
hiddenLossDrivers.mitigation: Implement SMED video analysis and parallel tasking.

hiddenLossDrivers.description: Large standard deviation indicates inconsistent procedures.
hiddenLossDrivers.impact: Causes unpredictable downtime and scheduling inefficiencies.
hiddenLossDrivers.mitigation: Standardize work instructions and train operators.

hiddenLossDrivers.description: Most setup tasks performed while machine is stopped.
hiddenLossDrivers.mitigation: Convert internal tasks to external using pre-kitting and tool staging.

hiddenLossDrivers.description: High number of changeovers per month reduces effective capacity.
hiddenLossDrivers.impact: Increases total downtime and reduces OEE.
hiddenLossDrivers.mitigation: Optimize production scheduling and batch sizes.

suggestedActions.action: Conduct SMED Kaizen event to reduce internal setup time by 50%.
suggestedActions.expectedImpact: Reduces changeover time from 45 to 22.5 minutes.

suggestedActions.action: Implement standardized work instructions for all changeover tasks.
suggestedActions.expectedImpact: Reduces standard deviation from 8 to 3 minutes.

suggestedActions.action: Create external setup checklists and pre-kitting stations.
suggestedActions.expectedImpact: Increases external setup ratio from 0.33 to 0.6.

suggestedActions.action: Use changeover matrix to group similar product families and reduce frequency.

dataConfidenceAdjusted.description: Confidence-adjusted optimization score based on input data completeness and variability.
